title: Trump declares US-Iran peace deal 'now complete,' as Iran confirms 'immediate end' to the war
subhed: The initial announcement from mediator Pakistan was quickly confirmed by Washington and Tehran, and an official signing was scheduled for June 19 in Switzerland. Trump said Sunday the Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for global oil and natural gas supplies, would open after the ceremony.

US imposes its domination on part of Latin America
subhed: Most Latin American countries, even non-aligned ones, have folded to the Trump administration's demands. Washington has not hesitated to use threats of sanctions or visa withdrawals to push its interests, with little regard for the rule of law.

title: Baudouin, the former king of the Belgians who was willing to do anything to stop abortion
subhed: Personal diaries and archives shed new light on the ethical and spiritual dilemmas faced by the former monarch in the years before his death, just as Belgium was preparing to legalize abortion.

title: Is it ever a good idea to... wear a beret?
subhed: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or else, to do so deliberately.

title: When Darwin comes to town: How the urban jungle disrupts animals' natural selection
subhed: Whether it's more aggressive chickadees, snails with lighter coloring or pigeons with anti-pollution plumage, urban wildlife is trying to adapt to city life. Is it behavioral flexibility or true genetic evolution? For the past 10 years, researchers have been investigating.

title: At Dior, Jonathan Anderson is thinking long-term, not 'trendy'
subhed: One year after this arrival at Dior, the 41-year-old Northern Irish designer is still going at full throttle, releasing collections for Uniqlo and his own brand JW Anderson in addition to his 10 for the luxury label. His goal, he says, is to 'transform' the brand.

title: Josh O'Connor, star of Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day,' wants to 'cultivate his garden'
subhed: At 36, he is one of the most sought-after actors in both independent cinema and major Hollywood productions. In just a few years, the British artist, who is passionate about gardening, has put his delicate masculinity and subtle acting style to work for a wide range of characters, from a lovesick farmer to Prince Charles or a priest.
